content.text:
This is a **Rust workspace** for a project called **Lens**. Here's what's in the current directory:

**Build / config**
- `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, `rust-toolchain.toml` — Rust workspace manifest, lockfile, and pinned toolchain
- `crates/` — the workspace member crates (the actual source)
- `target/` — build output
- `vendor/` — vendored dependencies

**Docs & agent setup**
- `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md` — shared and Claude-specific agent rules (per your project instructions, `AGENTS.md` is the entry point)
- `.agents/` — shared agent rules/index
- `docs/` — project documentation
- `spikes/` — exploratory/prototype code

**Tooling / meta**
- `.git/`, `.gitignore` — git repo
- `.claude/`, `.superpowers/`, `.opencode/`, `opencode.json` — agent harness configs
- `LICENSE`, `.DS_Store`

It's an early-stage Rust project (note the `spikes/` dir and foundation-related memory entries). Want me to dig into `AGENTS.md`, the `crates/`, or the `docs/` to get oriented on what Lens actually does?
Reason step by step about which directory you are in, then run a single shell command to print the working directory and list its files, then give me a one-sentence summary of what you found.

Reasoning: My earlier shell call reported `"cwd": "/Users/aakshintala/work/lens"`, and the project instructions (CLAUDE.md/MEMORY.md) are all rooted at `/Users/aakshintala/work/lens`, so that's my working directory. Let me confirm with a single command.

I'm in `/Users/aakshintala/work/lens`, the root of the **Lens** Rust workspace, containing its Cargo manifests/toolchain, source `crates/`, `docs/`, `spikes/`, build `target/`, vendored deps, and agent guide files (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`).

skills.name: installing-omnigent-from-source
skills.description: Use when you need a running omnigent server that matches the design's pinned 0.3.0.dev0 contract, when `omnigent --version` reports the wrong version (e.g. a 0.2.0 PyPI/uv release), or when (re)installing/updating omnigent for spikes, contract tests, or local runs.

skills.name: ask-matt
skills.description: Ask which skill or flow fits your situation. A router over the user-invoked skills in this repo.

skills.name: codebase-design
skills.description: Shared vocabulary for designing deep modules. Use when the user wants to design or improve a module's interface, find deepening opportunities, decide where a seam goes, make code more testable or AI-navigable, or when another skill needs the deep-module vocabulary.

skills.name: dev-lifecycle
skills.description: Structured SDLC workflow with 8 phases — requirements, design review, planning, implementation, testing, and code review. Use when the user wants to build a feature end-to-end, or run any individual phase (new requirement, review requirements, review design, execute plan, update planning, check implementation, write tests, code review).

skills.name: diagnosing-bugs
skills.description: Diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Use when the user says "diagnose"/"debug this", or reports something broken/throwing/failing/slow.

skills.name: domain-modeling
skills.description: Build and sharpen a project's domain model. Use when the user wants to pin down domain terminology or a ubiquitous language, record an architectural decision, or when another skill needs to maintain the domain model.

skills.name: find-skills
skills.description: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.

skills.name: grill-me
skills.description: A relentless interview to sharpen a plan or design.

skills.name: grill-with-docs
skills.description: A relentless interview to sharpen a plan or design, which also creates docs (ADR's and glossary) as we go.

skills.name: grilling
skills.description: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design. Use when the user wants to stress-test a plan before building, or uses any 'grill' trigger phrases.

skills.name: handoff
skills.description: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.

skills.name: improve-codebase-architecture
skills.description: Scan a codebase for deepening opportunities, present them as a visual HTML report, then grill through whichever one you pick.

skills.name: prototype
skills.description: Build a throwaway prototype to flesh out a design — a runnable terminal app for state/business-logic questions, or several radically different UI variations toggleable from one route.

skills.name: setup-matt-pocock-skills
skills.description: Configure this repo for the engineering skills — set up its issue tracker, triage label vocabulary, and domain doc layout. Run once before first use of the other engineering skills.

skills.name: tdd
skills.description: Test-driven development. Use when the user wants to build features or fix bugs test-first, mentions "red-green-refactor", or wants integration tests.

skills.name: teach
skills.description: Teach the user a new skill or concept, within this workspace.

skills.name: to-issues
skills.description: Break a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable issues on the project issue tracker using tracer-bullet vertical slices.

skills.name: to-prd
skills.description: Turn the current conversation into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker — no interview, just synthesis of what you've already discussed.

skills.name: triage
skills.description: Move issues and external PRs through a state machine of triage roles — categorise, verify, grill if needed, and write agent-ready briefs.

skills.name: writing-great-skills
skills.description: Reference for writing and editing skills well — the vocabulary and principles that make a skill predictable.
